title: gh#18: Fix: Media cleanup deletes storage before DB row — ordering risk
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/18.
Backlog section: P1 — Security & infra correctness.

## Problem

Cleanup loop deletes storage file first, then DB row. If the worker crashes between steps, the DB row persists pointing to a missing file. Reversing the order (DB first, then storage) is safer — an orphaned storage file is harmless, but a DB row pointing to a missing file confuses the UI.

Also, db.delete failures inside the loop are uncaught — one failing row aborts the entire batch.

## Identified by

Code quality review Run 2 — security-auditor (finding 3), error-handling-reviewer (finding 3)

## Fix

1. Delete DB row first, then storage (best-effort).
2. Wrap db.delete in try-catch with continue so remaining rows are processed.

## Files

- `packages/worker/src/media-cleanup-worker.ts:45-71, 83-105`
reproduction:
